title: Hydrostatic yaw system
abstract: The invention relates to a yaw system for a wind turbine (2), being adapted for rotatably supporting a nacelle (21) on a tower (20) of the wind turbine (2), the yaw system (1) comprising a rim (12), at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13'') slidably coupled to the rim (12), and at least one pre-tensioning device (14), wherein the at least one sliding pad (13, 3', 13'') and the rim (12) are pressed against each other by a pre- tensioning force (F P ) being at least partially exerted by the pre-tensioning device (14), wherein the yaw system (1) further comprises a hydrostatic system (15) operatively coupled with the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 3''), and adapted for exerting and maintaining a lifting force (FL) on the 1 sliding pad(13, 13', 13'') partially counteracting the pre-tensioning force (FP).
claims:
Claims We claim: A yaw system for a wind turbine (2), being adapted for rotatably supporting a nacelle (21 ) on a tower (20) of the wind turbine (2), the yaw system (1 ) comprising - a rim (12), - at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") slidably coupled to the rim (12), and - at least one pre-tensioning device (14), wherein the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") and the rim (12) are pressed against each other by a pre-tensioning force (F P ) being at least partially exerted by the pre-tensioning device (14), wherein the yaw system (1 ) further comprises a hydrostatic system (15) operatively coupled with the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13"), and adapted for exerting and maintaining a lifting force (FL) on the sliding pad (13, 13', 13") partially counteracting the pre-tensioning force (F P ). The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the sliding pad (13, 13', 13") comprises a sliding surface (13, 13', 13"0) that is in contact with a sliding surface (120) of the rim (12) 

 when the hydrostatic system (15) exerts the lifting force (F L ) on the sliding pad (13, 13', 13"). The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in claim 2, wherein at least one channel or chamber (13, 13', 13"1 ) is formed in the sliding surface (13, 13', 13"0) of the sliding pad (13, 13', 13"). The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in claim 3, wherein the cannel or chamber (13, 13', 13"1 ) is sealed against the rim (12). The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the hydrostatic system (13, 13', 13") comprises a device (150) adapted and arranged for creating a pressure between the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") and the rim (12) for generating the lifting force (F L ). The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the absolute value of the lifting force (FL) is significantly smaller, in particular smaller than 70 % of the absolute value of the pre-tensioning force (F P ). 7. The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the pre-tensioning force (F P ) is partially exerted by a weight of the nacelle (21 ). 8. The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in any of the preceding claims, the yaw system further comprising a control unit (18) adapted for receiving at least one input value and configured for increasing the lifting force (F L ) if the input value is above a predetermined threshold and decreasing the lifting force (F L ) if the at least one input value is below the predetermined threshold. The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in claim 8, the yaw system further comprising at least one drive motor (10) for moving the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") along the rim (12), wherein the control unit (18) is adapted for determining a torque of the drive motor (10) as input value. 10. The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in claim 8 or 9, the yaw system further comprising a sensor (180) adapted for determining a friction force of the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") along the rim (12), and configured to provide the friction force as input value to the control unit (18). 1 1 . The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in any of the preceding claims, the yaw system further comprising a plurality of sliding pads (13, 13', 13"), at least half of the plurality of sliding pads (13, 13', 13") being operatively coupled with the hydrostatic system (15). 12. The yaw system for a wind turbine as claimed in any of the preceding claims, wherein the pre-tensioning device (14) comprises at least one elastic element (140). 13. A wind turbine having a tower (20) and a nacelle (21) rotatably coupled to an upper end (200) of the tower (20) by means of a yaw system (1 ), the yaw system (1 ) comprising a rim (12) and at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") slidably coupled to the rim (12), wherein the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") and the rim (12) are pressed against each other by a pre-tensioning force (F P ), the yaw system (1 ) further comprising a hydrostatic system (15) operatively coupled with the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") and adapted for exerting and maintaining a lifting force (F L ) on the sliding pad (13, 13', 13") partially counteracting the pre-tensioning force (Fp). 14. The wind turbine according to according to claim 13, wherein the yaw system (1 ) is the yaw system (1 ) according to any of claims 1 to 12. 15. A method for adjusting a yaw angle (<p) of a nacelle (21) of a wind turbine (2), the method comprising the following steps: - providing (S100) a yaw system (1 ) being adapted for rotatably supporting the nacelle (21 ) on a tower (20) of the wind turbine (2), the yaw system (1 ) comprising a rim (12) and at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") slidably coupled to the rim (12), wherein the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13") and the rim (12) are being pressed against each other by a pre-tensioning force (Fp), the yaw system (1 ) further comprising a hydrostatic system (15) operatively coupled with the at least one sliding pad (13, 13', 13"); - exerting (S101 ) a lifting force (F L ) on the sliding pad (121 ) partially counteracting the pre-tensioning force (F P ) by means of the hydrostatic system (15); and - while exerting the lifting force (F L ), rotating (S102) the nacelle (21 ) around a yaw axis (z) with respect to the tower (20) of the wind turbine (2) for adjusting the yaw angle (<p). 16. The method according to claim 15, wherein the yaw system (1 ) is the yaw system (1 ) according to any of claims 1 to 12.
